message:
feat: implement Container integration for non-builtin commands

Architecture:
- Workspace DO uses ctx.container to spawn a real Linux environment
- Non-builtin commands (npm, node, etc.) routed to container-agent on :8080
- Source files sync via R2 FUSE mount (bidirectional, automatic)
- Build artifacts (node_modules, dist) on container local disk
- Snapshots to R2 as tar.zst on SIGTERM for fast restore

Container-agent (container/agent.mjs):
- POST /exec: spawn child process, return stdout/stderr
- GET /healthz: liveness check
- SIGTERM handler: tar.zst snapshots to R2 via S3-compatible API
- Boot: set up workspace symlinks (source -> R2 FUSE, builds -> local)

DO-side orchestration (workspace.ts):
- Container lifecycle: start, monitor, health check via alarm every 30s
- Index invalidation endpoint: container notifies DO of file changes
- Periodic reconciliation: alarm compares R2 list with SQLite index
- Single-writer guarantee: DO is sole writer to SQLite index
- Graceful fallback: returns "command not found" when no container

ProcessManager now accepts containerExec callback for routing.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+FROM node:22-slim
+
+# System tools for building and snapshot management
+RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
+    git curl jq zstd \
+    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
+
+# Container agent
+WORKDIR /opt/agent
+COPY agent.mjs ./
+COPY package.json ./
+
+# Workspace and local directories
+RUN mkdir -p /workspace /local /mnt/workspace
+
+EXPOSE 8080
+ENTRYPOINT ["node", "/opt/agent/agent.mjs"]
